description: Every remote/network call Pouchio makes, disclosed for the QVAC Hackathon. No AI inference is performed remotely — all inference (text generation + embeddings/RAG) runs on-device via @qvac/sdk. The only runtime network calls are Google authentication and the Gmail REST API (to read and send the user's mail). The QVAC SDK additionally fetches the AI model files once.

remoteCalls.purpose: Authenticate the user and obtain an OAuth access token used to call the Gmail API.
endpoints: GoogleSignin.getTokens() — retrieve a fresh access token
remoteCalls.dataSent: OAuth client ID and requested scopes; standard OAuth handshake. No email content.

remoteCalls.purpose: Read the signed-in user's mailbox and send mail. This is the app's core data source; it is NOT used for any AI.

endpoints.use: Fetch a message's headers/body for the card and reader
Mark a message read (remove UNREAD label)

remoteCalls.dataSent: For send: the user-composed message (To/Subject/Body). For reads: query params only.

remoteCalls.purpose: One-time download of the local AI model files, after which all inference runs offline on-device.
remoteCalls.dataSent: None — model files are downloaded; no user data is uploaded.
remoteCalls.ai: models for on-device inference (not remote inference)

notes: No third-party AI/cloud-inference API is used anywhere in the app.
Once the QVAC models are cached, all AI features keep working with no network (verifiable in airplane mode).
OAuth client IDs are EXPO_PUBLIC_* values and are public by design for native OAuth clients (no client secret).
